codeReview.summary: The implementation of POST /api/tasks/:id/claim and POST /api/tasks/:id/release is comprehensive and well-executed. The code properly handles all specified requirements: claim sets agent_id, claimed_at, and transitions status to in_progress from backlog/ready; returns 409 with current owner info when already claimed; validates agent existence (with auto-registration via ensureAgentExists); and release clears agent_id, claimed_at, and sets status to ready. The implementation includes proper error handling, input validation with Zod, and comprehensive test coverage with 16 test cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the AGENTS.md convention of placing route handlers in src/api/routes/ and using Zod for validation. The error response format { error: string, details?: any } is consistently applied.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/api/routes/tasks.ts:37-44, 400-409 - Error responses follow the documented pattern

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly uses the ensureAgentExists service for agent auto-registration, which aligns with the agent-auto-registration feature pattern. This is a good example of reusing shared business logic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/api/routes/tasks.ts:321 - await ensureAgentExists(agentId) called before claim operation

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker properly logs task operations using the logTaskClaimed and logTaskReleased service functions, integrating with the activity-logging feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/api/routes/tasks.ts:378 - await logTaskClaimed(id, agentId, previousStatus); src/api/routes/tasks.ts:427 - await logTaskReleased(id, releasingAgentId, 'ready')

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the backend-worker skill procedure correctly: created comprehensive tests first, implemented the endpoints with proper validation, and verified with both automated tests and manual curl checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 16 passing tests and manual curl verification for both claim and release endpoints

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation handles SQLite timestamp precision correctly by adjusting test expectations (subtracting 1 second to account for seconds-only precision). This knowledge could be documented for future workers.

The worker correctly implemented claimedAt date formatting with defensive checks for the 409 response, handling potential Date parsing edge cases. This shows good understanding of JavaScript Date handling with SQLite.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/api/routes/tasks.ts:340-354 - Safe date formatting with try/catch for claimedAt in 409 response

summary: The task-claim-release-api feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. The POST /api/tasks/:id/claim endpoint correctly sets agent_id, claimed_at, transitions status to in_progress from backlog/ready, returns 409 with current owner info when already claimed, and validates agent existence. The POST /api/tasks/:id/release endpoint correctly clears agent_id, claimed_at, and sets status to ready. The implementation includes 16 comprehensive tests covering happy paths, validation errors, and edge cases. No blocking or non-blocking issues were found. The code follows established conventions and integrates properly with existing services (agent auto-registration and activity logging).
